titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the André Alexis Papers
titlestmt.author: by Janet Lopes

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid is written in English.

conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Describing Archives: A Content Standard

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday June 26, 2025 at 23:03

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 55-58 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 59 (computer media): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ken Lopez Bookseller on the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund, 2022.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into five series: I. Writings, 1980-2020. II. Personal and professional papers, 1974-2022. III. Photographic prints and ephemera, 1994-1999. IV. Audiovisual material, 1956-1986. V. Computer media, 2001, undated.

bioghist.head: André Alexis
bioghist.p: André Alexis (1957- ) is an award-winning Canadian writer, novelist, playwright, and short story writer, who has also worked in radio, film, and opera. Born in Port of Spain, Trinidad and Tobago, Alexis grew up in Ottawa, Canada. His works include Childhood (1997), the Quincunx Cycle (2014-2021), Asylum (2008),and Pastoral (2014). Among many other honors, Alexis received a Windham-Campbell Prize (2017) and the Canada Reads prize (2017). Alexis currently lives in Toronto, Ontario and is an Adjunct Professor in the English and Creative Writing master's program at the University of Toronto.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: André Alexis Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The André Alexis papers contain materials related to the life and work of André Alexis including drafts, research notes, diaries, correspondence, photographic prints, audiovisual and born-digital material, as well as other related papers.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The André Alexis Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three sub-series: I. Published novels and collections, 1990-2020. II. Short stories, plays and other writings, 1980-2016. III. Essays, reviews and lectures, 1991-1999.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials in this series include drafts, essays, notes, reviews, and other writings by or related to André Alexis.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged chronologically by publication date.

did.unittitle: Despair and Other Stories of Ottawa (1994)
Master set, first pass pages, pages 55-207
Edited typescripts for "One Short Sleep" and "The Night Piece"
Edited typescripts for "The Night Piece", "Anabasis", and "Hunger: A Play"
Edited typescripts for "The Night Piece" and "One Short Sleep"
Front matter and correspondence with publisher
Manuscript, revisions, and laid in papers
Proof dust jacket, front panel and spine; two character timelines
Page proof with editorial and authorial edits, from Ottawa Citizen
Galley sheets, page and paragraph edits, with cover photos
Fourth draft, pages 127-204 with note from Liz Calder
Uncorrected proof copy, annotated, with laid in note and card
Lambton Kent , Henry Petroski, and other short notes
Copy, signed by middle school students
After first close edit, with Alexis' mother's comments
After first close edit, miscellaneous pages
Page proofs, London draft, heavily edited
Partial autograph manuscript of Asylum and short stories, "Kawabata" from Beauty and Sadness , journal
Review by T. F. Rigelhof, typescript
Chapter three to the end, plus Hidden Keys , autograph journal
First draft with edits and corrections
Autograph notes and manuscript in notebook
Short stories, plays and other writings

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged alphabetically by title.

did.unittitle: Adele in Two Days , typescript with correspondence declining submission
The Adventure of AB in C and other laid in typescripts
The Adventure of AB in C , Books 2-5
Alchemy: A Play in Two Acts and Stations: A Play in Two Acts , with sketches on manilla envelope
Ambit , two issues with pieces by André Alexis
The Andre Alexis and Sean Dixon Play by Alex Hugsley, typescript copy
"At Home with the Ladoceurs", "Fish Soup" and other short pieces
"Black Like Them: How Canadian Black Identity became a U. S. Culture Franchise", article in This magazine, two copies
Poems and laid in typescript of "Gatineau, 1989"
"Cigarettes", published in Toronto: B + A , 10th anniversary special issue
Contract for Despair and Other Ottawa Stories , resume, notes, several plays and short stories
Decalogue (2): Name in Vain (The Origin of Silence) , drafts
Decalogue (6): Don Giovanni and After , typescript with handwritten notes
The Decline of Philosphy in Ottawa, Ontario , manuscript and typescript
Defenestration , score and program, music by Veronika Krausas, text by André Alexis
Despair , novel excerpt with Alexander, "Dreams of Ribeiro", and various projects, loose sheets, typed
Dido and Aeneas play, "History: A Film in Two Parts and Stations rewrite, "Faith", and "Fire" for V. Krausas
Drawings in Ottawans in Exile Daily - homemade newspaper
Drafts of Autumn , "Paradise", "Mombassa," and other writings and letters
Drifts (play for radio), grant proposal, Renan's Nation and other projects
Faith , "Purgatory", submitted to Malahat Review, and "Lamp"

title.part: Family Happiness: A Play in Two Acts

did.unittitle: Family Happiness: A Play in Three Acts , typescript, and The Merchant's Son , film
Family Happiness: A Play in Three Acts , typescript photocopy
Fertility of Fire for Veronika Krausas and laid in Waterland: Monologue for Yuri Thomas
Fire: A Piece for Voice and Music , Mysteries: A Project for Radio and Ambition , typescripts
Fragments of plays with Leporello, Don Giovanni, and monks, typescripts
"Gardens of Stone", score with Veronika Krausas

History: A Monologue for Film ,
Mombassa by the Pavillion , and other writings
Holy Sonnets #10 , Hunger and other short pieces
Horse , Metaphysics of Morals , Childhood , and other works, typescripts
Impromptu , Animals Parasitic , drafts, and "Design of Neighborhood" sketch
Kuala Lumpur , The Night Piece , The Road , and other untitled typescripts
Leaving , handwritten notebook sheets and in-laid typescript
Letters (On a Book Lately Circulating in the Offices of Transport Canada , galley proofs
Letters (On a Book Lately Circulating in the Offices of Transport Canada , La Douceur Ladouceurs , and other writings
The Life of Henry Wing , abandoned novel of Trinidad
Marriage: A One Act Play , typescript
Mnemosyne by Veronika Krausas, collaboration with André Alexis, two copies
Merchants Son and laid-in typescripts of Kerouac , Southwestern Anabasis , and A Ghost Story
Typescripts, handwritten pages, and grant letter
Modernité , Paradis , and Lambton, Kent and other Vistas , typescripts
Monologues and stories, in-laid typescript of Weekend
Morningside , Rhubard , and Needs , second draft
Murderer, The Women's Hope , by Oskar Kokoschka, screen adaptation for "Puppets" by André Alexis and David Lewis
My Contemporaries , The Third Terrace , and other writings, manuscripts and in-laid typescripts
"My Secret Hiding Place: A Monologue for Radio", facsimile with corrections
Night typescript and title page for Hunger
Notes on Night (working title), sketch of stage, and other notes
Orpheus and Eurydice , score and libretto
"Ottawa (André)" notebook and "My Secret Hiding Place", in-laid radio monologue
Ottawa One , cardstock text and drawings by other authors
Parmenides Inc. and Family Happiness , laid in typescript
Petersburg: A Play and in-laid typescript page from The Third Terrace
Play (for Playwright's Unit), Easter , a play, and Soweto , a play
Radio Nomad, typescripts and images printed on paper
Radio Nomad Five , Radio Nomad Nine , and Radio Nomad Thirteen , typescripts with annotations
Radio Nomad Five: On Music , multiple partial drafts
Radio Nomad Three: Childhood , on country music, partial sheets
Road to Santiago de Compostela , The Third Terrace , and other typescripts
Score, Stone II , excerpts from the work of André Alexis
Short stories, review, first few pages of Childhood , radio plays, and other papers
Short Stories with Women of Southern Ontario , laid in typescript
Skvorecky play (film) and laid in typescript
"A Strange Incident", "History: A Short Film", and other work
The Third Terrace , The Metaphysics of Morals , and miscellaneous pages, typescript drafts
Three Women , two typescript copies with edits
The Three-Day Novel: An Anthology , includes "W: or the Twelve Rooms", and laid-in typescript of Alfred the Great
Travels , a play, loose sheet about the play Family Happiness and "Play", V and Marj. characters
Travels in Autumn and in Winter , laid in typescript, translated by André Alexis
Typescripts of short stories, essays, and other writings including Restless , Night As Memory , and "On Prayer: An Essay in Three Parts"
Untitled play featuring André and Sean
"Untitled: A Work in Progress", Mombassa by the Pavilion , and two sets of pages of My Street: A Monologue for Radio , typescripts
W: Or the Twelve Poems , handwritten notebook sheets
Waterland , script for CD production, drafts of Testimony , and other writings
Weekend , Lo Que Puede un Sastre! , and other works
"What is a Short Story?" and typescript pages, laid-in
Where Do We Live? , four drafts
Where Do We Live? , notes, and review of The Piano Man's Daughter by Timothy Findley

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged alphabetically by title.

did.unittitle: Comments on books as judge of Governor General's Award
D. H. Lawrence: The Story of a Marriage , review by André Alexis
The Evolution of Racism , review by André Alexis
Manuscript Found in Saragossa , review by André Alexis
Rene Char (essays, reflections, notes), books from library, and papers related to Hawthorne's Notebooks
Reviews of "Juneteenth", "More Matter" and other papers
Proposal to record self reading David Hume's A Treatise on Human Nature , an essay on stone, and laid in scores for Stone and Shelter
Shift magazines, includes laid in clippings and reviews by or related to André Alexis
The Solitary Twin , typescript by Harry Mathews with André Alexis' comments
Then We Take Berlin , review by André Alexis
Victorian Love Stories , review, and other writings and notes

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two sub-series: I. Personal papers, 1978-2017. II. Professional papers, 1974-2022.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence, notes, photographs, artwork and other materials related to André Alexis's life and work.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged alphabetically by title.

did.unittitle: Ambition , other writings and personal papers
Correspondence from Catherine Bush, John Metcalf, and Alexis writing home from London and Florence
Correspondence re: real estate and other papers
Journal entries and travel notes in Italy
Notebook of personal thoughts on literature and writing
Notes from agent, postcard and other papers
Notes and writings related to ghost stories
Notes and writings related to National Gallery Museum visit in London, Kant, and reflections on being a writer
Notebook titled "Sheila Willis"- lists, notes, and diary entries
Travels to London and Florence, with laid in maps

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged alphabetically by title.

did.unittitle: Acceptance letter from Event magazine for short story "Letters"
Acceptance letter from Martin Bax of Ambit and response from André Alexis
Canadian Stage Company, letter of agreement
Correspondence from Ice Records related to permission to use lyrics in novel
Correspondence from Richard Sanger, with typescript of "The Mountain Muse"
Correspondence from Toronto Arts Council regarding grant for Childhood
Correspondence regarding restlessness, youth, and nomadism
Despair contract and agreement for opera production
First Conference for Black Canadian Writers, correspondence and related papers
Fresh Blood: Canadian New Gothic Anthology , contract and ten pages of lists in French
Grant criteria from Ontario Arts Council and Toronto Arts Council
Grant letter from Ontario Arts Council
The Great Canadian Sonnet , poems by David McFadden
House of Commons certificate honoring Fifteen Dogs
Invitation to a conference at Clarion University
"The Lost River" broadside poem by Michael Redhill
"Mapping the Writer: An Appreciation of 'Naked Lunch'" by Tom Lackey
Maps of Toronto waterfront for book
Poster for Home and Rude Circus
Program for Name in Vain (Decalogue Two)
Rejection letter from Martin Bax, Ambit magazine
Reviews of Dared & Done and Then We Take Berlin
Sexual difference, ontological difference and other writings
Typescripts, correspondence, drawings, and other related papers
The Utopian , play by Thomas Bernhard, photocopy
What is a Nation? , speaker biographies and notes
Zeno: A Proposal for a Pocket Musical , and notes in notebook

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographic prints of André Alexis, events, and other individuals, along with related materials.

did.unittitle: Photographic prints of André Alexis and negatives
Photographic print of gravesite monument from Cape Cod
Mounted cover of Despair inscribed to André Alexis from Linda Watson

did.materialspec: The program starts abruptly. There's a lip sync error at 00:07:05 that last for more than two minutes. Loss of video at 00:22:05 for 20 seconds. Ends with audio but no video.
